author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Resolved. Split ferray-autodiff/src/lib.rs (was 2291 lines) into five focused modules plus a dedicated tests file, matching the layout proposed in the issue:

  lib.rs          53 lines — module declarations, crate-level docs, public re-exports
  dual.rs        252 lines — DualNumber struct, constructors, Display, arithmetic ops (dual-dual and dual-scalar), PartialOrd
  functions.rs   529 lines — 30+ differentiable methods on DualNumber + 16 flat free-function mirrors (sin/cos/exp/...)
  num_impls.rs   420 lines — all num_traits impls: Zero, One, ToPrimitive, FromPrimitive, NumCast, Num, and the big Float trait (just glue)
  api.rs         122 lines — derivative, gradient, slice-based jacobian convenience API
  array_ops.rs   430 lines — Array-aware forward-mode AD (landed earlier for #534)
  tests.rs       967 lines — all three pre-existing test modules (unit/finite_difference/proptests), renamed to avoid module_inception

No file over 1000 lines. Public API is unchanged — lib.rs does pub use to re-export everything that used to live at the crate root, so existing callers (including the tests, ferray-core, and downstream crates) don't need any changes. Tests were moved verbatim with s/use super::*/use crate::*/ and needed num_traits imports added back for the unit test module.

112 lib tests + 15 integration tests + 9 doctests all pass — same counts as before the split. Clippy clean. Full workspace builds.
